content_blocks.text:
पुत्रो वटुर्दिव्यजनाधिको घनी विराटयोगोत्रवयोधिको धुना ।
मेषादिके पुष्करभागसंज्ञिका मुहूर्तजन्मादिषु शोभनप्रदाः ॥ ५८ ॥

verse_data.sanskrit_text:
पुत्रो वटुर्दिव्यजनाधिको घनी विराटयोगोत्रवयोधिको धुना ।
मेषादिके पुष्करभागसंज्ञिका मुहूर्तजन्मादिषु शोभनप्रदाः ॥ ५८ ॥

content_blocks.text:
The degrees 21, 14, 18, 8, 19, 9, 24,
11, 23, 14, 19, and 9 attained by the Moon in Mesha
and the following signs respectively are called Puth-
kara and are to be taken as producing auspicious effects
when a muhurtha, a birth or other such points are under
consideration

verse_data.translation:
The degrees 21, 14, 18, 8, 19, 9, 24,
11, 23, 14, 19, and 9 attained by the Moon in Mesha
and the following signs respectively are called Puth-
kara and are to be taken as producing auspicious effects
when a muhurtha, a birth or other such points are under
consideration

content_blocks.text:
58. These Pushkarabhagas are referred to in the text in
Adhyaya VII, Sloka 25 infra.

The inhabited regions denoted by
Mesha and the other signs are respectively (1) Patala
(2) Karnata (3) Chera (4) Chola (5) Pandya (6) Kerala
(7) Kollasa (8) Malaya (9) Saindhava (10) North Panchala
(11) Yavana and (12 Kosala.

verse_data.translation:
The inhabited regions denoted by
Mesha and the other signs are respectively (1) Patala
(2) Karnata (3) Chera (4) Chola (5) Pandya (6) Kerala
(7) Kollasa (8) Malaya (9) Saindhava (10) North Panchala
(11) Yavana and (12 Kosala.

content_blocks.text:
स्वाम्याशाख्यं यत्तदाहुः प्लवत्वं भानुक्रान्तादंषुसंज्ञाभिजित्स्यात् ।
होरातन्त्रे पारिषदाताभिधाने संज्ञाध्यायः कीर्तितो राशिशीलः ॥६१॥

verse_data.sanskrit_text:
स्वाम्याशाख्यं यत्तदाहुः प्लवत्वं भानुक्रान्तादंषुसंज्ञाभिजित्स्यात् ।
होरातन्त्रे पारिषदाताभिधाने संज्ञाध्यायः कीर्तितो राशिशीलः ॥६१॥

content_blocks.text:
The point of the compass which is
assigned to the lord of a sign indicates the characteristic
of Plava or slope of the sign. The name of the fourth
house from that occupied by the Sun is called Abhijit.
In the astrological work called 'Parijata,' the chapter
defining the "Properties of Signs" has been propounded.

verse_data.translation:
The point of the compass which is
assigned to the lord of a sign indicates the characteristic
of Plava or slope of the sign. The name of the fourth
house from that occupied by the Sun is called Abhijit.
In the astrological work called 'Parijata,' the chapter
defining the "Properties of Signs" has been propounded.

raw_text:
Sl. 68-61
प्रथमोऽध्यायः
36
पुत्रो वटुर्दिव्यजनाधिको घनी विराटयोगोत्रवयोधिको धुना ।
मेषादिके पुष्करभागसंज्ञिका मुहूर्तजन्मादिषु शोभनप्रदाः ॥ ५८ ॥
Sloka. 58. The degrees 21, 14, 18, 8, 19, 9, 24,
11, 23, 14, 19, and 9 attained by the Moon in Mesha
and the following signs respectively are called Puth-
kara and are to be taken as producing auspicious effects
when a muhurtha, a birth or other such points are under
consideration
NOTES
58. These Pushkarabhagas are referred to in the text in
Adhyaya VII, Sloka 25 infra.
क्रमात्पाटलकर्णाटचरचोलवसुन्धराः ।
पाण्ख्यकेरल कोल्लास मलयाबनिसैन्धवाः ।। ५९ ।।
उदक्पाञ्चालयवनकोशलक्षितिसंज्ञकाः ।
मेषादिसर्वराशीनां वासदेशाः प्रकीर्तिताः ।। ६० ॥
Slokas. 59-60. The inhabited regions denoted by
Mesha and the other signs are respectively (1) Patala
(2) Karnata (3) Chera (4) Chola (5) Pandya (6) Kerala
(7) Kollasa (8) Malaya (9) Saindhava (10) North Panchala
(11) Yavana and (12 Kosala.
स्वाम्याशाख्यं यत्तदाहुः प्लवत्वं भानुक्रान्तादंषुसंज्ञाभिजित्स्यात् ।
होरातन्त्रे पारिषदाताभिधाने संज्ञाध्यायः कीर्तितो राशिशीलः ॥६१॥
Sloka. 61. The point of the compass which is
assigned to the lord of a sign indicates the characteristic
of Plava or slope of the sign. The name of the fourth
house from that occupied by the Sun is called Abhijit.
In the astrological work called 'Parijata,' the chapter
defining the "Properties of Signs" has been propounded.
NOTES.
61. यत्तदेवप्नवत्यं is another reading.
